text.title: Refactoring Patterns Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure for one refactor cycle: identify smell, ensure tests, transform, verify, commit.

step.name: identify-smell
step.input: lint output / coverage gap / review comment
step.action: Read smell. Map to canonical transform via 06-decision-tree.xml. If smell crosses >5 files → escalate to [[code-decomposition-patterns]] and exit this methodology.

step.name: ensure-tests
step.action: Run scope test suite. If green AND covers affected paths → proceed. If green but coverage gap → add characterisation tests in a separate commit, then proceed. If red → fix tests first (separate task), do not refactor on red.
step.decision-gate: If characterisation tests would themselves require >100 LOC: stop and request human review — refactor target may be too entangled for safe automated work.

step.name: apply-transform
step.action: Apply ONE transformation. Use IDE/language-server tooling for renames. For Extract Method: select the lines, invoke extract, name by intent. For Replace Conditional with Polymorphism: introduce strategy/visitor; move branches into subclasses one at a time.

step.name: verify
step.action: Run scope test suite again. If green → tests_green_after = true; proceed. If red → revert, re-plan; do NOT push.

step.name: commit
step.action: Commit with title `refactor: <transform-name> in <area>`. Pre-commit hook `refactor-scope-guard.sh` rejects if >5 files. Record playbook entry per 02-output-contract.xml schema.
